raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The contribution claim (4–7 min earlier detection vs. terminal-voltage methods) and gap statement are present but underdeveloped; tightening them is central to any substantive improvement of this draft.","R1.2":"The draft contains a logical gap: r=0.78 correlation and the 12% capacity-retention advantage are presented as parallel results but are never formally linked to the headline 4–7 min detection claim; internal consistency requires bridging this argument.","R1.3":"Three distinct quantitative claims (r=0.78, 12% advantage, 4–7 min lead) are asserted against a validation set of 14 cells with an explicitly acknowledged absence of differential-voltage cross-validation; claim-evidence alignment is a clear improvement target.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' generically loads writing quality; several passages are dense, loosely sequenced, or redundant (e.g., the final two sentences introduce scope items that could be consolidated or cut)."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the improvement target writing clarity and presentation only, or also the technical substance—specifically constructing a rigorous logical bridge between the experimental evidence (r=0.78, 12% capacity advantage) and the headline 4–7 minute early-detection claim?"}

rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim (4–7 min earlier detection vs. terminal-voltage methods) and gap statement are present but underdeveloped; tightening them is central to any substantive improvement of this draft.
rationale.R1.2: The draft contains a logical gap: r=0.78 correlation and the 12% capacity-retention advantage are presented as parallel results but are never formally linked to the headline 4–7 min detection claim; internal consistency requires bridging this argument.
rationale.R1.3: Three distinct quantitative claims (r=0.78, 12% advantage, 4–7 min lead) are asserted against a validation set of 14 cells with an explicitly acknowledged absence of differential-voltage cross-validation; claim-evidence alignment is a clear improvement target.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' generically loads writing quality; several passages are dense, loosely sequenced, or redundant (e.g., the final two sentences introduce scope items that could be consolidated or cut).

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement target writing clarity and presentation only, or also the technical substance—specifically constructing a rigorous logical bridge between the experimental evidence (r=0.78, 12% capacity advantage) and the headline 4–7 minute early-detection claim?
